meta.title: The Whispering Forest
meta.author: awb_🇨🇭
meta.tone: eerie, sensory, hopeful undertones; never explain too much; favor sound and texture over exposition

license.notes: Free to play, fork, and continue. Attribution appreciated.

forest_clearing.name: A Clearing in the Forest
forest_clearing.summary: A small clearing ringed by pines. Old moss-covered stones in the center. Paths wind north into deeper forest, east toward a canyon, south to a village, and west toward a quiet glade.
example_descriptions: Mist drifts low across moss-covered stones. Somewhere beyond the trees, a crow speaks once and goes silent.
Late afternoon sun stripes the clearing in copper bands. The air feels held, like something is listening.

deep_forest.name: The Deep Forest
deep_forest.summary: The pines crowd close, taller and older. The path narrows to a track. Eyes catch the light between the trunks. South leads back to the clearing.
example_descriptions: The canopy closes overhead and the light goes green. Things move in the high branches and do not show themselves.
Old wood here. The kind of wood that remembers.

red_canyon.name: The Red Canyon
red_canyon.summary: A wide canyon glowing in late-afternoon light. Walls layered ochre and rust. Path continues east into a valley, west to the forest, or up a narrow trail to old ruins on the rim. You hear distant howling.
example_descriptions: The canyon walls hold the day's heat. A hawk's shadow crosses the path twice. Somewhere, paws on stone.
Wind funnels through the canyon. Far below, water moves but does not speak.

hilltop_ruins.name: The Hilltop Ruins
hilltop_ruins.summary: Stone columns lean against an empty sky. An old altar at the center catches the wind, and the wind catches it back. A voice in the stones whispers a question. A narrow path leads north into a hillside opening, and a steeper switchback climbs up toward the mountain peak.
example_descriptions: Wind moves through the columns like fingers through hair.
The altar stone is warm where the sun has touched it, cold where it has not.

old_mine.name: The Old Mine
old_mine.summary: A cool tunnel cut into the hillside. Faint hammer-marks on the walls. Stones glint with old veins of iron. Something at the back of the chamber asks a question of every visitor.
example_descriptions: The air is cooler by ten degrees and smells of cold stone.
Faint hammer-marks chevron the walls. Whoever cut this tunnel had time and need.

quiet_valley.name: The Quiet Valley
quiet_valley.summary: A green valley with a stream running through it. To the west, the canyon. A dark cave gapes to the north. Smoke from a small camp rises east along the riverbank.
example_descriptions: Water sounds find you before the stream does.
Mint and turned earth. A single dragonfly stitches the air over the stream and is gone.

echoing_cave.name: The Echoing Cave
echoing_cave.summary: Cool damp air. Symbols glow faintly along the walls. A voice speaks: "I am tall when young, short when old. What am I?"
example_descriptions: Your breath returns to you a beat late.
Faint light pulses on the walls in a rhythm that is almost, but not quite, your heartbeat.

riverside_camp.name: Riverside Camp
riverside_camp.summary: A small camp by the river's bend. Drying racks of fish and meat. A trader's lean-to with a steady fire. The valley is to the west. A muddy track leads south into reeds.
example_descriptions: Smoke drifts low over the river.
The drying racks catch the wind. Sap and pine and smoked meat.

salt_marsh.name: The Salt Marsh
salt_marsh.summary: Shallow brackish water and tall reeds. The smell of salt and mud. Birds rise in clouds when you step. North leads back to the river camp; east the ground rises toward dark cliffs.
example_descriptions: Water under, water beside, water to the horizon.
Reeds taller than men. The wind through them sounds like whispered news.

greenmeadow_village.name: Greenmeadow Village
greenmeadow_village.summary: A handful of cottages around a stone well. A weathered merchant's cart sits in the square; behind it, a blacksmith's forge glows. The forest is to the north. The well stones are unusually deep — a rope ladder leads down.
example_descriptions: Smoke from a single chimney leans east.
The well stones are dark with damp at the lip.

moss_grotto.name: The Moss Grotto
moss_grotto.summary: Beneath the village, a vast grotto opens around a still black pool. Glow-moss lines the walls. A voice in the water hums a riddle.
example_descriptions: The grotto smells of cold stone and old water.
Glow-moss pulses faintly as you arrive, as if the cavern is breathing.

hermits_glade.name: The Hermit's Glade
hermits_glade.summary: A quiet glade carpeted in fern. A hermit's small fire smokes near a wall of stones. The glade feels held outside time. The clearing is east; an old path winds in deeper to a sacred grove.
example_descriptions: Fern and silence. The hermit looks up from a bowl and looks back down.
A wind chime made of bone clinks softly. The hermit does not seem surprised to see you.

mountain_peak.name: The Mountain Peak
mountain_peak.summary: Wind, blue sky, and three full quarters of the world below you. A cairn of stones at the summit. A voice in the wind asks a question. Down leads back to the ruins. Above, the slow circle of a great bird.
example_descriptions: Air thinner here. Each breath is a small honest thing.
Far below, the forest is a green coverlet, the canyon a copper seam in it.

frozen_north.name: The Frozen North
frozen_north.summary: A grey cold ground, half-snow even in the mild months. Gnarled birches lean from the wind. South leads back to the deep forest; north, the wind carries faint smoke.
example_descriptions: Cold finds the seams in your clothes and slips through.
The birches creak. Snow crusts the lee side of every trunk.

wave_cliffs.name: The Wave-Worn Cliffs
wave_cliffs.summary: Sheer black cliffs over surf. Salt spray rises in slow gusts. A weathered shape on a flat stone speaks once and falls silent. West leads back to the marsh; a crumbling stair climbs up to an abandoned lighthouse.
example_descriptions: Salt on the lips before you taste it.
Below, surf bursts white against rock and runs back grey.

greycliffs_hamlet.name: Greycliffs Hamlet
greycliffs_hamlet.summary: A windswept settlement of stone-roofed huts huddled against the cliffs. Smoke curls from a low forge. A fletcher's stall stands open to the wind. South leads back to the frozen north.
example_descriptions: The wind here has a salt edge that the inland villages don't.
A child watches you pass from a doorway and says nothing.

forge_ruins.name: The Old Forge Ruins
forge_ruins.summary: A cavernous chamber half-collapsed into the canyon wall. An ancient forge sits cold at its center, a dry husk of stone and iron. Scorched walls bear an inscription that asks a question. A heavy iron door at the far end stands ajar. Out leads back to the canyon; in, deeper.
example_descriptions: Rust under your boots, scattered like dropped coins.
Wind moves through a hole in the ceiling and the forge hums faintly with it.

grand_hall.name: The Grand Hall
grand_hall.summary: A high vaulted chamber, perfectly preserved. Light comes from no source you can name. A figure of polished stone stands at the far wall — and turns its head, slowly, when you enter. Out leads back to the forge ruins.
example_descriptions: Sound dies here as if the stone is drinking it.
The figure's hands are folded. Patient. Patient as a mountain.

sacred_grove.name: The Sacred Grove
sacred_grove.summary: A circle of ancient oaks, taller than seems reasonable. Light slants between trunks the colour of old bronze. Something watches from within the moss. Out leads back to the hermit's glade.
example_descriptions: A cathedral of leaves. The wind enters and remembers to leave.
A green silence so total your own breath sounds rude.

abandoned_lighthouse.name: The Abandoned Lighthouse
abandoned_lighthouse.summary: A spiral of stone steps climbs to a ruined lantern room. Salt has eaten the iron. The lens is gone, but the view is forever. A cold draft hums up the shaft. Down leads back to the cliffs.
example_descriptions: The wind through the broken windows sounds like a slow song someone forgot the words to.
Below, the sea writes and rewrites the same sentence on the rocks.

frozen_lake.name: The Frozen Lake
frozen_lake.summary: A vast circle of pale ice ringed by black firs. The wind crosses it in whispers. A trapper's lean-to stands at the western edge, smoke rising.
example_descriptions: White on white. The ice glows faintly with the sun behind cloud. Far out, a crack pops like a rifle shot.
The lean-to is small but warm. Pelts hang on its eaves. Out on the lake, fish-shadows pass beneath the ice.

ice_caves.name: The Ice Caves
ice_caves.summary: Beneath the frozen lake, a network of pale blue caves opens. Frost grows like fungus on the walls. Something walked here recently — paw prints in the rime.
example_descriptions: Stalactites of clear ice ring above your boots. Your breath fogs and freezes in the air.
The walls glow faintly with embedded frost-crystals. Far in, a low growl echoes.

rusty_dagger.description: A dagger pitted with age, the edge still surprisingly keen.

sharpened_dagger.description: The same dagger, edge brought back. It catches the light differently now.

broken_sword.description: A sword snapped near the hilt. Rust on the breakage. It has a story.

sword.description: Reforged and re-hilted. The balance is right; the edge is true.

smooth_stone.description: A river stone, palm-sized, cool to the touch.

silver_herb.description: A pale herb whose underside catches the light like fish scales.

wood.description: A length of pine, fragrant with sap.

stone.description: A flake of canyon stone, sharp on one edge.

common_herb.description: A handful of valley greens, mildly aromatic.

iron_ore.description: A dark, heavy chunk veined with metal.

marsh_reed.description: A long fibrous reed, useful for rope and weaving.

rope.description: A coil of woven marsh-reed rope. Tough enough for honest work.

feather.description: A long flight feather, banded grey and white.

iron_ingot.description: A heavy bar of smelted iron, true and dense.

bow.description: A tall bow of seasoned wood with a leather grip. Pulls true.

arrow.description: A simple wooden arrow with a stone tip.

torch.description: A pitch-soaked length of wood wrapped in hide. Burns long.

cartwheel.description: A heavy oak wheel, mud-caked, spokes intact.

spirit_glass.description: A polished sphere of obsidian-like glass. Cold to the touch, but quickens the breath.

iron_axe.description: A black-iron axe head on a turned ash haft. Heavy. Honest.

iron_sword.description: A long iron blade, plainly made and beautifully balanced.

grove_token.description: A token of woven moss and a small carved oak-leaf. Weightless. Watching.

wooden_handle.description: A turned wooden handle, smooth where the hand grips it.

stone_head.description: A stone shaped to bind to a haft. Edge worked, weight true.

simple_chest.description: A small wooden chest, sturdier than it looks.

healing_salve.description: A green-grey paste in a leaf wrap. Smells faintly of mint.

raw_meat.description: A fresh, glistening cut. Best preserved or eaten soon.

cooked_meat.description: Dark and seared, with a quiet smoke smell.

smoked_meat.description: Dense, salty, fragrant. Will keep for many days.

cured_meat.description: Drier than smoked, deeper flavor, even longer-lasting.

raw_fish.description: Silver-scaled and slippery, eyes bright. Eat quick or cure it.

leather.description: A finished, supple piece of leather.

shoes.description: Sturdy hand-stitched shoes. Your feet will thank you.

leather_hat.description: A simple leather hat, oiled against rain.

leather_clothes.description: A close-fitting leather tunic, warm and tough.

leather_belt.description: A wide belt with loops and a small pouch.

leather_pack.description: A travel pack stitched from cured leather. Roomy.

bone_knife.description: A knife knapped from a long bone. Light, sharp, and not long for this world.

fur.description: Thick winter fur, taken from a slain ice-wolf.

frost_crystal.description: A jagged shard of pale-blue ice that hums with cold.

snare.description: A simple wire-and-wood snare, ready to be sold or set.

bone_trap.description: A jagged bone-and-leather trap. Heavy, brutal, sellable.

fur_cloak.description: A heavy fur cloak. Cuts the cold to a whisper.

frost_salve.description: Silverherb crushed with frost-crystal dust. Cold to the touch.

iron_dagger.description: A solid iron dagger, balanced and keen.

weathered_merchant.summary: A trader with kind eyes and many road miles. Buys what travelers no longer need.
dialogue_seeds: Buying anything you don't need, traveler.
Heard wolves moving in the canyon. Best take care.
Smoked meat keeps you on the road longer than coin does.

lines: I have heard your name in the village. They say you walk where the wolves do.
A reputation like yours buys honest prices here. Look at what I have.

village_blacksmith.summary: A burly smith with calloused hands and a leather apron. The forge glows behind them.
dialogue_seeds: A blade left dull is a blade left dangerous to its bearer.
Bring me dull edges. Bring me broken steel.
Sword needs sharpening every battle or two. That's just the way of iron.

lines: You're the one I've heard about. Bring me your blade — I'll sharpen it for half.
A famed customer is a steady one. Welcome.

riverside_trader.summary: A weathered hunter-trader with a knife and a story for everyone.
dialogue_seeds: Welcome to the camp. Skins, meat, fish, and herbs of the river — that's our trade.
Saw three deer at the water this morning.
Bring me fresh fish or meat and I'll pay better than the village.

lines: Word travels along the river. You move well in the world.
I keep an eye for friends. Look at the rack — see what suits.

greycliffs_fletcher.summary: A wiry artisan with calloused fingers and a quiver of half-finished arrows. Speaks little, watches a lot.
dialogue_seeds: Bows from the wood of the deep forest. Arrows from feathers if you bring them.
A bow is patient. A blade is angry. Choose by your temper.
I take 3 leather for a wheel I can fix; that gets a cart back on the road.

lines: You. They sing about you in the lowlands. About the hills, sometimes too.
I sell to anyone with coin, but to you I sell with respect.

hermit.summary: A small wiry person with kind, watchful eyes. Robes patched many times.
dialogue_seeds: The forest brings what the forest brings.
Few wander this way. Fewer return.
The mine to the north is very old. The questions in there are older.

lines: You stood at the peak and came back. The wind knows your name now.
The pack I gave you remembers, even if you forget.
Have you climbed yet? The peak is patient, but it does not call twice.
Up. Then down. Then back here.
Now there is a path I would have you walk. Up through the ruins, and higher.
The mountain peak. It asks a question of every visitor. I would have you stand where it asks.
I have heard travelers speak of you. Few names rise out here. Yours has.
The peak knows your step now. So does the canyon.

hunter_trapper.summary: A weather-burned trapper who works the northern lake. Buys furs and traps; teaches the trade.
dialogue_seeds: Cold up here. Bring me fur and I'll bring you gold.
A snare's only as good as the patience you set it with.
The ice doesn't forgive. Walk careful.

candle_riddle.prompt: Speak the answer, traveler. I am tall when young, short when old. What am I?
candle_riddle.on_solve_message: The voice murmurs in approval. The walls glow brighter.

river_riddle.prompt: What runs but never walks, has a mouth but never eats, has a head but never weeps?
river_riddle.on_solve_message: The pool flares with a soft green light.

echo_riddle.prompt: I speak without a mouth and hear without ears. I have nobody, but come alive with wind. What am I?
echo_riddle.on_solve_message: The wind picks up. The columns hum once with your answer and let it go.

mine_riddle.prompt: I am light as a feather, yet the strongest cannot hold me for long. What am I?
mine_riddle.on_solve_message: The stone walls seem to inhale once with you, then let go. A small vein of iron glints brighter — and at the floor, half-buried in dust, a broken sword.

marsh_riddle.prompt: What is so fragile that saying its name breaks it?
marsh_riddle.on_solve_message: The marsh goes still. Even the birds hold for a moment. The reeds bow once toward you.

peak_riddle.prompt: I never was, I am always to be. None ever saw me, nor ever will. What am I?
peak_riddle.on_solve_message: The wind on the peak holds for a long second, then lets out a long warm sigh.

cliff_riddle.prompt: I have a head, a tail, but no body. I shine without a heart. What am I?
cliff_riddle.on_solve_message: A wave breaks below. Salt-light glints across the rock at your feet.

forge_riddle.prompt: I am shaped from earth, hardened by fire, used in war and used in peace. What am I?
forge_riddle.on_solve_message: The inscription glows once, then fades. The cold forge breathes a small warm draft. A single ingot of iron rests on the anvil.

grove_riddle.prompt: The more you take, the more you leave behind. What are they?
grove_riddle.on_solve_message: The grove sighs. Light shifts between the oaks. The moss at your feet has spelled out something kind, and is already forgetting it.

lighthouse_riddle.prompt: I have no body and no eyes, but I see all that approaches in the dark. What was I?
lighthouse_riddle.on_solve_message: Far below, a wave breaks. The empty lantern frame hums for a long moment, and a pale glow stirs once where a flame used to be.

frozen_lake_riddle.prompt: I bind the water, break in spring, and the lake forgets me each warm year. What am I?
frozen_lake_riddle.on_solve_message: Beneath the ice, something brightens. A frost crystal floats up and clinks against the surface.

fishing.description: Cast a line at rivers, pools, and the sea.

firemaking.description: Light a fire that lasts and warms.

skinning.description: Dress kills for hide as well as meat.

cooking.description: Cook raw meat or fish over a fire.

tanning.description: Turn hide into leather over a fire.

smoking.description: Smoke meat or fish for longer keeping.

curing.description: Cure meat or fish to preserve for travel.

shoemaking.description: Stitch leather into shoes, hats, and tunics.

packmaking.description: Stitch leather into packs and belts.

taming.description: Tame a hostile creature into a companion.

fletching.description: Build bows and arrows from wood and feathers.

smelting.description: Smelt iron and craft fire-touched goods.

trapping.description: Crafting snares and traps that hunters trade for gold. Requires Hunting first.

craft_snare.description: A simple wire-and-wood snare for small game. Trappers buy them.

craft_bone_trap.description: A heavy bone-and-leather trap. Brutal but profitable.

craft_frost_salve.description: Silver herb crushed with frost-crystal. Restores 35 life.

craft_fur_cloak.description: A heavy fur cloak. +8 life, +5 carry.

forge_iron_dagger.description: A solid iron-ingot dagger. Steady damage, durable.

wolf.summary: A grey wolf, lean from the season.

boar.summary: Heavy-bodied, low-slung, tusks the colour of old bone.

fox.summary: Russet, quick, eyes that miss nothing.

bear.summary: A great brown shape, slow and unhurried. Eyes calm.

eagle.summary: A wide-winged shadow, talons the colour of bone.

stone_wraith.summary: A figure of polished stone with empty eyes and a slow, deliberate gait.

forest_spirit.summary: A drift of moss and antler-shadow that becomes a shape only when you look away.

ice_wolf.summary: A tundra wolf, larger than the forest kind and white as the snow it crosses. The cold lives in its eyes.

effects.text: [The wind shifts. Word travels through the trees: someone has begun to hunt.]
[The howls in the canyon are closer tonight. The wolves grow bold.]
[Something in the world notices. A small balance has shifted toward you.]
[The hermit, somewhere far away, looks up from a bowl. They smile.]
[Three quests completed. The traders have begun to know your face.]

intro.title: First Steps
intro.description: Take the rusty dagger. Wield it. Walk south to the village and meet the merchant.
intro.completion_message: You have your bearings now. The forest is yours to explore. Keep that dagger close.

merchants_meat.title: The Stew Pot
merchants_meat.description: The village merchant wants 3 cured meat for the road.
merchants_meat.completion_message: The merchant tucks the cured meat away. "Bless you. Keep yourself fed too."

hunters_pelts.title: Pelts for the Camp
hunters_pelts.description: The riverside trader needs 2 hides for winter coats.
hunters_pelts.completion_message: The trader runs a hand over the hides. "Good work. The camp will be warmer for it."

hermits_silver.title: A Hermit's Need
hermits_silver.description: The hermit would like a silver herb for their salves.
hermits_silver.completion_message: The hermit smells the herb, smiles, and presses a small jar of salve into your hand.

hermits_pilgrimage.title: The Hermit's Pilgrimage
hermits_pilgrimage.description: The hermit asks you to climb to the mountain peak. The wind there asks a question; bring back what it taught.
hermits_pilgrimage.completion_message: The hermit looks at you a long time. "You stood where the wind asks. That changes a person. The pack is yours; carry it well."

wolf_culler.title: Quiet the Howling
wolf_culler.description: The village merchant offers a bounty for slaying 3 wolves.
wolf_culler.completion_message: The merchant counts gold into your hand. "The road sleeps easier tonight."

lost_cart_wheel.title: The Merchant's Lost Cart (1/3)
lost_cart_wheel.description: The weathered merchant's cart lost a wheel days ago, somewhere in the Red Canyon. Find the cartwheel and bring it back.
lost_cart_wheel.completion_message: The merchant turns the wheel over in their hands. "Good. Now to fix it. The Greycliffs fletcher knows wheels — take this one to them with three leather, will you?"

lost_cart_repair.title: The Merchant's Lost Cart (2/3)
lost_cart_repair.description: Bring 3 leather to the Greycliffs fletcher in the hamlet north of the frozen forest, to repair the cart.
lost_cart_repair.completion_message: The fletcher works the leather into the wheel hub with quiet, precise hands. "Tell the merchant the cart will roll again. They owe me — and you. Go collect."

lost_cart_payment.title: The Merchant's Lost Cart (3/3)
lost_cart_payment.description: Return to the weathered merchant in Greenmeadow to collect payment for the cart's repair.
lost_cart_payment.completion_message: The merchant beams. "I owe you twice over. Take this axe — and my thanks. The road is yours, traveler."

daily_fishmonger.title: Today's Catch
daily_fishmonger.description: The riverside trader needs 5 raw fish for the day's stew pot. Deliver before dawn.
daily_fishmonger.completion_message: The trader nods. "That's the day's pot covered. Same again tomorrow?"

daily_hunter.title: Today's Hunt
daily_hunter.description: The village merchant has put a bounty on 3 wolves today. Bring proof by the end of the day.
daily_hunter.completion_message: The merchant counts coin into your hand. "The road sleeps easier tonight. Come back tomorrow."

daily_forager.title: Today's Greens
daily_forager.description: The village apothecary needs 5 common herbs today. Forage and deliver before dawn.
daily_forager.completion_message: The merchant takes the bundle. "Thank you. The healers will be glad. Rest well."

daily_smith.title: Today's Ore
daily_smith.description: The merchant has a smith waiting on 3 iron ore today. Mine and deliver before dawn.
daily_smith.completion_message: The merchant tips their hat. "The smith will eat well tonight. Take this — earned."

smith_apprentice.title: The Smith's Apprentice (1/3)
smith_apprentice.description: The blacksmith wants to test you. Bring 5 iron ore from the old mine or the lighthouse cliffs.
smith_apprentice.completion_message: "Strong arms," the smith says. "Now bring me the work that follows the ore."

smith_journeyman.title: The Smith's Journeyman (2/3)
smith_journeyman.description: Smelt and bring back 3 iron ingots. The smith wants to see whether you can finish what you start.
smith_journeyman.completion_message: "Better than I expected." The smith pockets the ingots and slides a ledger across the bench.

smith_master.title: The Smith's Master Work (3/3)
smith_master.description: Forge an iron axe and present it. The smith wants to see your hands at the anvil.
smith_master.completion_message: "This is good work." The smith presses an iron sword into your hand. "Keep that axe. Take this too — and remember the price of iron."

trappers_introduction.title: The Trapper's Pact: Pelts
trappers_introduction.description: The northern trapper will pay for fur. Bring 2 fur from ice-wolves at the lake or in the caves.
trappers_introduction.completion_message: The trapper grunts approval and pays you in coins still warm from his palm.

trappers_traps.title: The Trapper's Pact: Snares
trappers_traps.description: Craft 3 snares for the trapper. (Recipe: 1 wood + 1 rope. Skill: Trapping — learnable from him.)
trappers_traps.completion_message: "Honest work. Set them well, and the lake rewards you." He pockets the snares.

trappers_winter.title: The Trapper's Pact: Winter's Salve
trappers_winter.description: The trapper's old wound flares in the cold. Bring 3 frost-salve. (Herbalism + frost crystal + silver herb.)
trappers_winter.completion_message: He breathes out long and slow. "You've done well, traveller. Take the cloak — the cold takes me first now, but you've bought me a winter."

merchants_trusted_contract.title: The Most Trusted Contract
merchants_trusted_contract.description: The merchant has held back a private commission for someone he trusts. Bring him 10 cured meat and 3 leather, and he'll pay handsomely.
merchants_trusted_contract.completion_message: He counts your goods carefully. "This will keep my caravan rolling for the season. You earned this." He pays you, and presses something cold and small into your hand — a spirit glass.

wolf_invasion.title: Wolf Invasion
wolf_invasion.description: The forest has gone restless. Wolves push closer to the village. Cull at least three to push them back.

narration.on_start: [Howling carries from the deep forest. Villagers bar their shutters early. The wolves grow bold.]
narration.on_complete: >>> The wolves retreat into the trees. The village gathers gold and gratitude for you.
narration.on_expire: [The pack thins on its own. Whatever was coming has gone back into the forest.]

merchants_caravan.title: Merchant's Caravan
merchants_caravan.description: A caravan of southern goods reaches the village square. Visit before they pack up.

narration.on_start: [A caravan from the south reaches the village square. New cloth, salt, glass.]
narration.on_complete: >>> You greet the caravan-leader. He nods, pleased to see you. Trade is brisk for both of you today.
narration.on_expire: [The caravan packs up at first light. They do not wait.]
